name: Calls
description: Integrates instant voice calling into workspace

sections.title: General settings
sections.subtitle: Settings for participants, screen sharing, ringing, and more

settings.help_text: When Test mode is enabled, only system admins can start calls in channels. This allows for testing to ensure calls are working as expected.
The maximum number of participants that can join a call. If left empty or set to 0, an unlimited number of participants can join.
When set to true, participants in a call can share their screen.
When set to true, screen sharing simulcast is enabled. This can help improve screen sharing quality.
When set to true, ringing feature is enabled: participants in DMs or GMs will receive a desktop alert and a ring notification when a call is started. Changing this setting requires a plugin restart.

When set to true, AV1 codec is enabled to encode screen sharing tracks. This may improve screen sharing quality for clients that support it.
Note: this setting won't apply when EnableSimulcast is set to true.

When set to true, clients will use WebRTC data channels to signal new media tracks. This can make the process more efficient and less prone to race conditions, especially upon frequent WebSocket disconnects.
When set to true, video calls are enabled in direct message channels.

sections.title: RTCD service
sections.subtitle: Configure a custom service used to offload calls and efficiently support scalable and secure deployments

settings.help_text: (Optional) The URL of a running RTCD service instance that will host calls. When set to a non-empty value, all calls will be handled by the external service.

sections.title: RTC server
sections.subtitle: Network settings for the built-in RTC server

settings.help_text: The local IP address used by the RTC server to listen for UDP connections.
The local IP address used by the RTC server to listen for TCP connections.
The UDP port the RTC server will listen on.
The TCP port the RTC server will listen on.
When set to true, the RTC service will run in dual-stack mode, listening for both IPv6 and generating candidates in addition to IPv4 ones.
(Optional) The IP address that will be used as an ICE host candidate. If empty, it will default to being resolved via STUN.

(Optional) A port number to be used as an override for host candidates instead of the one used for listening.
Note: this port will be applied to host candidates for both UDP and TCP.
(Optional) A list of ICE server (STUN/TURN) configurations to use. This field must contain a valid JSON array.
(Optional) The secret key used to generate short-lived auth credentials for TURN.
(Optional) The number of minutes generated TURN credentials will be valid for.
(Optional) When enabled, configured TURN candidates will be passed and used for connections initiated by the server.

sections.title: Call recordings
sections.subtitle: Recordings include the entire call window view along with participants’ audio track and any shared screen video. Recordings are stored in Mattermost

settings.help_text: (Optional) When set to true, call recording is enabled.
The URL of a running calls-job-service instance used for recording calls.
The maximum duration in minutes for call recordings. The value must be within the [15, 180] range.

The audio and video quality for call recordings.
 Note: this setting can affect the overall performance of the job service and the number of concurrent recording jobs that can be run.

sections.title: Call transcriptions
sections.subtitle: Allows turning calls into transcription files. Recordings must be enabled

settings.help_text: (Optional) When set to true, post-call transcription is enabled.
Speech-to-text API to use for post-call transcription.
The speech-to-text model size used for post-call transcription. Larger models produce more accurate results at the cost of processing time and resource usage.
The number of threads used by the post-call transcriber. The value must be within the [1, numCPUs] range.
API key for Azure speech services
API region for Azure speech services

sections.title: Live captions
sections.subtitle: Shows spoken words as text labels during a call. Recordings and transcriptions must be enabled

settings.help_text: (Optional) When set to true, live captioning is enabled.
The speech-to-text model size used for live captions. Larger models produce more accurate results at the cost of processing time and resource usage.

The number of separate transcribers for live captions per call. Each one transcribes one audio track at a time. The result of LiveCaptionsNumTranscribers * LiveCaptionsNumThreadsPerTranscriber must be within the [1, numCPUs] range.

settings.display_name: Live captions num threads per transcriber
settings.help_text: The number of threads per transcriber for live captions. The result of LiveCaptionsNumTranscribers * LiveCaptionsNumThreadsPerTranscriber must be within the [1, numCPUs] range.
The language passed to the live captions transcriber. It must be a two-letter language code as per ISO 639 Set 1, e.g. 'en'. If left empty it will default to English 'en'.
When Test mode is enabled, only system admins can start calls in channels. This allows for testing to ensure calls are working as expected.

The TCP port the RTC server will listen on.

Note: this port will be applied to host candidates for both UDP and TCP.

(Optional) The secret key used to generate short-lived auth credentials for TURN.
